THE GOAL
CUSTOMER RETENTION
‘Make new friends, but keep the old.
One is silver, the other gold.’
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 7 --

What Does Client Want To Think
(Be Shown or Told)
They are receiving incredible work
We are working tirelessly for their business
They aren’t paying us too much
(perceived value)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 7 --

THE clients assumption
When clients are paying this sort of money and they’re not in
our field, it’s easy for them to assume that our work is a lot
easier than it actually is. They also want to believe that every
single minute of our time is being spent with them. It’s also very
important for them to believe that they are getting special
treatment. Lastly, they must perceive the service to be
underpriced.
The client will never value our work to the true extent it
deserves. We must work around this with what I refer to as the
‘4 Pillars of Value’
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 7 --

THE PILLARS
There are 4 a month, one weekly.
Creative Planner 	BRAND REpORT 	FEEDBACK FORM	GENERAL REPORT
Each pillar represents a piece of value.
Here are a few examples:
WEEK 1 	WEEK 2 	WEEK 3 	WEEK 4
Every week the client needs to receive a new piece of value.
Some examples are listed here:
Creative Planner
Draft Content
Final Content
A Report
IAG Document (Research Paper, etc.)
Brand Report (Tailored for them)
Feedback Form
Long, personalised email, message, etc
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 7 --

If you don’t have a value giving proposition ready for that week,
then you fucking make one. There should never be a week
where the client goes without receiving one of these.
Apart from the 4 Pillars of Value, the client should also receive
check ins, these can be short emails or messages (depending on
the client). This is just to baby-step them through the process
and keep them updated on what work we are doing.
Check Ins:
Monday + Thursday Weekly
Furthur Infomation
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
